item.title: Agile + Offshore: Making It Work for Japanese Clients
item.description:
One question I get a lot: Can Agile work in offshore delivery for Japanese clients?

Short answer: Yes, but you need to adapt.

What works:

  1. Visual communication — Japan teams love kanban boards. Use them religiously.
  2. Fixed-price sprints — Japanese clients prefer predictability. Scrum with fixed scope per sprint builds trust.
  3. BrSE as bridge — Having a bilingual PM who understands both Agile and Japanese business culture is crucial.
  4. Overcommunicate — Daily standups are non-negotiable. Weekly demo even for internal releases.

Common pitfalls:

item.title: Welcome to My Corner
item.description:
After spending over a decade in tech — from designing pixels to managing P&Ls — I finally set up a proper corner of the internet.

What you’ll find here:

  • Thoughts on Agile, project delivery, and offshore management
  • Lessons from working across Japan, UK, US, and Vietnam teams
  • My journey learning Japanese while managing ODC projects
  • Occasional design and product musings

Stay tuned. 🚀

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Son Nguyen — a Senior Project Manager with a designer’s eye and an Agile mindset.

I bring order to chaos, keeping cross-cultural projects on track and helping Agile teams actually enjoy the process. PMI-ACP® certified with 10+ years bridging tech and business across Japan, UK, US, and Vietnam.

What I do:

  • Lead offshore delivery teams (Japan/Vietnam)
  • Translate between business stakeholders and engineering teams
  • Drive Agile adoption beyond the buzzwords
  • Speak English, Vietnamese, and learning Japanese (N4→N3 track)

Career goal: Leading bigger challenges, pushing Agile beyond the buzzword, and delivering projects that make both clients and teams proud.
